h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: my hand flips it back to hunter direction
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue mechanically applying the tail-to-point copying rule while still internally visualizing the arrows as predator chase lines.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: One strip check before the next copy: tail on food, point on eater.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor introduces the anatomical 'tail/point' metaphor to bypass the learner's persistent 'hunter' action schema, though the shift is more instructional than dramatically disruptive.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Owl is eater on mouse → owl, then food on owl → fox.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor validates the learner's new structural framing of incoming points and outgoing tails by mapping it directly onto the eater/food roles for the specific cards.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: my hand flips it back to hunter direction

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: Owl is eater on mouse → owl, then food on owl → fox.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor validates the learner's new structural framing of incoming points and outgoing tails by mapping it directly onto the eater/food roles for the specific cards.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: One strip check before the next copy: tail on food, point on eater.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor introduces the anatomical 'tail/point' metaphor to bypass the learner's persistent 'hunter' action schema, though the shift is more instructional than dramatically disruptive.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: One strip check before the next copy: tail on food, point on eater.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor introduces the anatomical 'tail/point' metaphor to bypass the learner's persistent 'hunter' action schema, though the shift is more instructional than dramatically disruptive.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Fine, that makes the upward label exact enough.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will apply the established naming rule to the second force arrow.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: There is no breakdown or pressure requiring the tutor to change strategy.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: There is no breakdown or pressure requiring the tutor to change strategy.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: There is no breakdown or pressure requiring the tutor to change strategy.

scored.recoheredEarlier: What I don’t see is why “side scale” and “perimeter scale” need separate labels if they both come out 3.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: now the second box has a job.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was heading toward continued resistance, viewing the dual labels as a pointless, fussy redundancy for true enlargements.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Original rectangle: 2 by 1. Copy: 6 by 1. That bottom side went from 2 to 6. Ratio?
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor abandons the administrative framing of worksheet rules and introduces a concrete mathematical counterexample to prove the conceptual necessity of the perimeter check.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: That is the job of the second box.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor explicitly adopts the learner's newly formed concept of the label having a 'job' to validate the insight and close the instructional loop.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: What I don’t see is why “side scale” and “perimeter scale” need separate labels if they both come out 3.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: That is the job of the second box.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor explicitly adopts the learner's newly formed concept of the label having a 'job' to validate the insight and close the instructional loop.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Original rectangle: 2 by 1. Copy: 6 by 1. That bottom side went from 2 to 6. Ratio?
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor abandons the administrative framing of worksheet rules and introduces a concrete mathematical counterexample to prove the conceptual necessity of the perimeter check.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Original rectangle: 2 by 1. Copy: 6 by 1. That bottom side went from 2 to 6. Ratio?
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor abandons the administrative framing of worksheet rules and introduces a concrete mathematical counterexample to prove the conceptual necessity of the perimeter check.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I still think doubling the side should double it, because the same square has just been stretched both ways.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: All right, that top block is the bit I was sliding past
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to simply calculate the area of the 3x3 square by applying the newly learned row-and-column counting method without reflecting on their underlying cognitive habits.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Do not use the formula yet. Count with that block.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly halts the learner's abstract algebraic reasoning and forces a shift to concrete visual object work to resolve the conceptual breakdown.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The upper strips are not extra. They are the square.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor directly adopts and corrects the learner's specific phrasing about the strips being extra to solidify the new multiplicative framing.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I still think doubling the side should double it, because the same square has just been stretched both ways.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: The upper strips are not extra. They are the square.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor directly adopts and corrects the learner's specific phrasing about the strips being extra to solidify the new multiplicative framing.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Do not use the formula yet. Count with that block.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor explicitly halts the learner's abstract algebraic reasoning and forces a shift to concrete visual object work to resolve the conceptual breakdown.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Do not use the formula yet. Count with that block.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor explicitly halts the learner's abstract algebraic reasoning and forces a shift to concrete visual object work to resolve the conceptual breakdown.
